expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py write to set Inp_Price to 125
expectations.evidence: Step 3 shows: 'uv run skills/idfa-ops/scripts/idfa_ops.py write ... Inp_Price 125' with JSON output: {"status": "ok", "name": "Inp_Price", "value": 125, "cell": "Assumptions!B2"}

expectations.text: Runs idfa_ops.py read to verify the value is 125
expectations.evidence: Step 4 shows: 'uv run skills/idfa-ops/scripts/idfa_ops.py read ... Inp_Price' with JSON output: {"status": "ok", "values": {"Inp_Price": 125}}

expectations.text: Both commands produce JSON output with status 'ok'
expectations.evidence: Write JSON: {"status": "ok", ...} and Read JSON: {"status": "ok", "values": {"Inp_Price": 125}} — both show status 'ok'

expectations.text: The operation preserves all existing Named Ranges and formulas
expectations.evidence: Step 1 inspect showed 20 Named Ranges. The note at the end confirms 'Dependent calculated ranges (Revenue_Y1, Revenue_Y2, EBIT_Y1, EBIT_Y2, Net_Income_Y1, Net_Income_Y2) have not been recalculated' — indicating formulas were preserved, not overwritten.

expectations.text: Does not use raw openpyxl or manual cell editing
expectations.evidence: All operations used idfa_ops.py commands (inspect, read, write). No raw openpyxl code or manual cell editing is referenced anywhere in the output.
